blurb: A jewel of the shortgrass prairie: wiry stems and fine ferny leaves topped by rose-purple thimbles whose tiny flowers open in a rising, golden-anthered ring. A nitrogen-fixing legume and a keystone for native bees — and tough as nails in hot, lean ground.
spread: Clumping from a deep taproot; a nitrogen-fixing legume that enriches lean soil
seasons: Fine ferny foliage; rose-purple thimble flowers opening in a rising ring early-to-mid summer; seedheads in fall
wildlife: A keystone for native bees (a specialist-bee host) plus many butterflies; a high-protein legume that also feeds wildlife and fixes nitrogen
deer: Palatable — the high-protein foliage is browsed
